skill.name: obsidian-helper
skill.description: Obsidian Helper guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Obsidian note workflows. It helps create daily notes, capture ideas, and generate periodic reviews through the Obsidian MCP integration.
skill.summary: Use AI-assisted daily notes, capture workflows, and reviews inside an Obsidian vault.
skill.author: Claude Code

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but most are Markdown examples, Dataview syntax, or local setup instructions rather than executable skill code. Confirmed risks are medium severity: the skill guides broad Obsidian vault reads and writes, asks users to edit Claude Code MCP config, and handles a local API key. No evidence found for malicious exfiltration, remote command-and-control, hidden payloads, or prompt injection aimed at bypassing marketplace review.

medium_findings.title: Broad Obsidian Vault Read and Write Access
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The skill depends on MCP tools that list, read, search, append, and patch Obsidian vault content. This is legitimate for the advertised workflow, but it exposes private notes and can modify user data if misused.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented required tools explicitly include vault listing, file reads, batch reads, searches, appends, and patches. The risk is semantic and intentional rather than hidden malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Shell Setup Writes Claude Code MCP Configuration
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The setup guidance includes terminal commands that create a Claude configuration directory and append MCP server configuration. This is a real local configuration change, though it is presented as user-run setup documentation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible setup instructions and include writes under the Claude configuration path. They do not show dynamic command injection or covert execution by the skill.

medium_findings.title: Local API Key Stored in MCP Environment Configuration
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The skill instructs users to place an Obsidian Local REST API key in MCP environment configuration. This is expected for the integration, but it creates secret-handling risk if the config is shared or exposed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly mention copying an API key and setting OBSIDIAN_API_KEY. The endpoint is local, so there is no evidence of exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Code Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. Many external command findings are Markdown command names, slash commands, Dataview examples, or fenced examples. They are not executable Ruby backticks in source code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown tables or fenced documentation blocks. No runtime language file or executable backtick evaluation is present there.

low_findings.title: Dataview Time Fields Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptography findings in Dataview references correspond to file metadata fields and query examples. No hashing or cryptographic algorithm usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is Dataview query syntax for sorting and filtering Obsidian notes. I did not find evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar weak crypto use.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Network References Are Local or Dependency Links
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The network findings are a local Obsidian REST endpoint and public dependency documentation links. No remote data upload or credential transmission target is shown.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The IP address is loopback and the README URLs identify Obsidian plugin and npm package dependencies. No suspicious external endpoint is present in the reviewed files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem-Capable Note Automation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill automates reading and writing Obsidian vault files through MCP tools. This pattern is useful but requires user trust because vault notes may contain private information.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented tool list and reference examples directly show read, search, append, and patch operations over vault files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Configuration and Secret Setup
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup instructions guide users to write Claude Code MCP configuration and include an Obsidian API key. This should be documented as a local setup risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The lines explicitly describe collecting the API key and writing MCP configuration. The risk is local secret exposure, not confirmed credential exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Obsidian users often spend time moving ideas, tasks, and reviews across notes. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured workflows for daily notes, captures, and periodic reviews.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether the Obsidian MCP connection is available before running note workflows.
Creates or updates daily notes and carries forward unfinished tasks from recent daily notes.
Searches existing notes before capturing new content, then asks before appending to a related note.
Generates daily, weekly, or monthly review reports from recent notes and task patterns.
Uses a PARA and Zettelkasten vault structure with a small frontmatter field whitelist.
Provides Dataview and Tasks query examples for vault dashboards and reviews.
limitations: Requires Obsidian, the Local REST API plugin, and the mcp-obsidian server to be configured.
The workflow can read and write vault content through MCP tools, so users should review changes.
The setup guidance stores a local Obsidian API key in Claude Code MCP configuration.
Most workflow text is written in Chinese, so English-only teams may need adaptation.

use_cases.title: Start a daily note
use_cases.description: Create today's daily note, bring forward unfinished tasks, and keep a consistent journal structure.
use_cases.target_user: Obsidian users who plan their day in notes

use_cases.title: Capture research ideas
use_cases.description: Search related notes before saving a new idea, then append it to the best matching location.

use_cases.title: Review recent work
use_cases.description: Summarize notes, completed tasks, unfinished items, and active themes for weekly or monthly reflection.

prompt_templates.title: Create today's note
prompt_templates.prompt: Use obsidian-helper to start today's daily note. Check yesterday's unfinished tasks and ask before adding extra sections.

prompt_templates.title: Capture a quick idea
prompt_templates.prompt: Use obsidian-helper to capture this idea: {{idea}}. Search for related notes first and ask where to save it.

prompt_templates.title: Build a weekly review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use obsidian-helper to generate a weekly review from recent daily notes. Include task counts, themes, achievements, and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Refine vault workflow rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Use obsidian-helper to review my vault structure and suggest safe capture, review, and frontmatter rules that match PARA and Zettelkasten.

output_examples.output: A daily note is created or updated with today's focus areas, inherited unfinished tasks, and space for morning, afternoon, and evening notes.

output_examples.input: Capture a note about REST API naming.
output_examples.output: The assistant searches related notes, recommends a matching note if found, and asks before appending the new capture.
The assistant summarizes recent daily notes, task completion, active files, achievements, insights, and suggested priorities for next week.

best_practices: Keep the Local REST API plugin limited to trusted local workflows.
Review suggested write locations before allowing the assistant to append vault content.
Use the whitelist frontmatter fields unless your vault has a documented schema.
anti_patterns: Do not grant vault access when the Obsidian Local REST API token is shared or exposed.
Do not let the assistant rewrite whole notes when an append or patch is enough.
Do not use this skill for sensitive vaults without confirming every read and write action.

faq.question: Does this skill require Obsidian MCP?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed around Obsidian MCP tools and the Local REST API plugin.

faq.question: Can it create new notes?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create or append notes through MCP tools when the user confirms the workflow.

faq.question: Can it read private notes?
faq.answer: Yes, if the configured MCP server can access the vault. Use it only with trusted vaults.

faq.question: Is the setup fully automatic?
faq.answer: No. The user must install Obsidian dependencies and configure the local API key.

faq.question: Does it support English prompts?
faq.answer: Yes, but the skill instructions and user guidance are primarily written in Chinese.

faq.question: Does it send data to a remote service?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The documented network endpoint is a local Obsidian API address.
